name: Glazed Venison Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Mince all three meats finely and add them to a bowl.
2. Add the zest of the orange and lemon, crushed garlic, thyme, olive oil, vinegar, a generous pinch of powdered bay leaves, and plenty of black pepper.
3. Mix thoroughly and stir in the wine. Cover and leave to marinate overnight.
4. Season with salt (1 teaspoon is usually sufficient). Fry a small nugget of the mixture to check the seasoning.
5. Turn the pate into a terrine with a capacity of about 2 1/4 pints.
6. Pack the mixture well into the corners of the dish and use a spoon to hollow out slightly the center top.
7. Cover with greaseproof paper and foil, then place the dish in a roasting pan containing enough hot water to come halfway up the sides of the dish.
8. Bake at 325°F (160°C) gas mark 3 for 2-1/4 to 2-1/2 hours.
9. Using a bulb baster, remove and reserve most of the juices that surround the pate.
10. Replace the greaseproof paper and foil, then lightly press the pate with weights ranging from 1-1/2 to 2 pounds.
11. Cool for 1-1/2 hours.
12. Drain off any remaining juices that have not been re-absorbed by the pate.
13. Mix all the venison juices collected with the juice of the orange, adding water if necessary to make a total of 1/2 pint.
14. Dissolve the gelatine powder in the mixture and use it to glaze the pate, adding a few bay leaves and kumquats for decoration if desired.
